fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer dietitian license to north carolina, dietitian compact north carolina, north carolina dietitian endorsement application

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annual (by March 31) + 30 CE hours per 2 years

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN) — Category A
license_types.description: Full licensure for individuals currently registered with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) as an RDN. The primary credential for practicing dietetics in North Carolina.

license_types.title: Provisional Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (PLDN) — Category B
license_types.description: A 12-month provisional license for individuals who have completed an ACEND-accredited program and supervised practice and are preparing to take the CDR exam. Initial fee is $133. Cannot be renewed.

license_types.title: Licensed Nutritionist (LN) — Category F
license_types.description: For individuals holding a Certified Nutrition Specialist (CNS) or Diplomate of the American Clinical Board of Nutrition (DACBN) credential.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Create a new user account on the NCBDN Licensure Portal (https://ncbdn.elicensure.net/) and complete the online application for initial licensure.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $130 through the NCBDN Licensure Portal as part of your online application.

steps.title: Submit Education Verification
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your degree-granting institution to be sent directly to the North Carolina Board of Dietetics/Nutrition.

steps.title: Provide Supervised Practice & CDR Registration Proof
steps.description: Ensure verification of your supervised practice (e.g., CDR Verification Statement) and proof of current CDR registration (passing the RDN exam) are submitted to the board.

steps.title: Complete Background Check & Declaration
steps.description: Complete a criminal background check through the approved vendor (IdentoGO) and submit a Declaration of Primary Residence, both as instructed by the NCBDN.

steps.title: Application Review
steps.description: The board will review your completed application once all required materials are received. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 weeks, but may vary.

documents.detail: Must show a master's or doctoral degree in nutrition or a related field from a regionally accredited institution.
Proof of current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (for Category A applicants).
A clear copy of a valid, government-issued identification.
Completed authorization form and associated $38 fee.

provisional_text: North Carolina offers a Provisional Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (PLDN) credential under Category B. This is for individuals who have completed an ACEND-accredited academic program and supervised practice and have received an 'Authorization to Test' email from CDR. The provisional license is valid for 12 months, costs $133, and cannot be renewed. The applicant must pass the CDR exam within that period to upgrade to a full LDN license.
reciprocity_text: North Carolina offers licensure by reciprocity under Category D for applicants currently licensed in another state whose qualifications do not fit into other categories (non-RD, non-CNS). Category D does not have an online application; applicants must contact the NCBDN directly. All RD/RDNs applying from out of state should apply under Category A, the standard pathway. The Board may grant a license if the other state's requirements are substantially the same as North Carolina's.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost for a Category A (RDN) license is $248, which comprises a $60 application fee, a $150 issuance fee, and a $38 criminal background check fee. Annual renewal is $95.

faqs.question: When does the North Carolina dietitian license expire?
faqs.answer: All North Carolina dietitian/nutritionist licenses expire on March 31st each year, regardless of when they were issued. Annual renewal must be submitted between January 1st and March 31st.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for NC dietitians?
faqs.answer: Licensees must complete 30 hours of continuing education every two years. For RDNs (Category A), meeting the CDR's continuing education standards (75 hours per 5-year cycle) fulfills the state requirement.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in North Carolina. To work in North Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 3 Business Days.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: North Carolina offers licensure by reciprocity under Category D for applicants currently licensed in another state whose qualifications do not fit into other categories (non-RD, non-CNS). Category D does not have an online application; applicants must contact the NCBDN directly. All RD/RDNs applying from out of state should apply under Category A, the standard pathway. The Board may grant a license if the other state's requirements are substantially the same as North Carolina's.
